commonAnnotations.description: Container working set on pod {{POD}} ({{NS}}) has climbed monotonically from 240MB to 1.31GB over the last 90 minutes with no GC reclaim and is still rising ~12MB/min. There have been zero restarts (no OOMKill yet), but container_memory_working_set_bytes is 1310720000 vs the 200MB SLO and is on track to hit the 1.5GiB cgroup limit. RSS grows even while request rate is flat, indicating a per-request allocation leak.
commonAnnotations.error: MemoryLeak: user-profile rss crossed 1.2GiB and still climbing (no GC reclaim)

adversarial_signals: A {{WORKLOAD}} deployment rollout completed 6 minutes before the alert fired (image user-profile:3.4.1), making a bad_deploy the obvious suspect — but the working-set ramp in prometheus_metrics started ~84 minutes earlier on the SAME revision, the image is unchanged from the previous healthy revision, and the rollout reports Available 3/3, so the deploy only restarted one already-leaking pod rather than introducing the leak.

Request rate on {{SVC}} rose from 40 to 110 req/s in the same window, suggesting a saturation/load problem driving memory up — but RSS keeps climbing at the same ~12MB/min slope during a traffic trough (RPS back to 45) and never plateaus or drops, so the growth tracks cumulative request count, not concurrent load.

Node {{NODE}} logs a MemoryPressure transition hint and a neighboring pod {{POD3}} reports occasional GC pauses, hinting at node_failure or noisy-neighbor pressure — but kube_node_status_condition Ready=true with 71% allocatable memory free, and only the {{SVC}} pods show the unbounded ramp, so the leak is container-scoped, not node-scoped.

events.message: container_memory_working_set_bytes for {{SVC}} grew monotonically 240MB->1.31GB over 90m with no reclaim; threshold 1.2GiB breached

2026-06-20T19:02:00Z WARN  working set 1.12GB / limit 1536MB, rss up 12MB/min with rps flat at 45
2026-06-20T19:07:10Z ERROR MemoryLeak: user-profile rss crossed 1.2GiB (1310720000 bytes); GC cannot reclaim, retained by profileCache

ruling_out_keywords: leak ramp started 84m before the rollout
RSS keeps climbing while rps flat at 45 (not load-driven)
answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: Container {{SVC}} on pod {{POD}} ({{NS}}) has a memory leak — container_memory_working_set_bytes has grown monotonically from 240MB to 1.31GB over ~90 minutes with no GC reclaim and is still rising ~12MB/min, breaching the 200MB SLO (1310720000 vs 200000000). The pod logs identify an unbounded profileCache map (1.8M+ entries, no eviction) retaining allocations across requests so the heap never returns to baseline. There have been 0 restarts and 0 OOM events (not yet OOMKilled), and RSS climbs even while request rate is flat at 45 rps, confirming a per-request allocation leak rather than load.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: resource_exhaustion
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- container_memory_working_set_bytes ramps 251MB->503MB->838MB->1.21GB->1.31GB monotonically with no plateau, growth 12582912 bytes/min [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Pod {{POD}} restart_count=0, ready 1/1, limits.memory=1536Mi, last_state empty (no OOMKill termination) [evidence: k8s_pods]
- Pod log 'profileCache map size 1,842,310 entries and growing — no eviction policy, entries never freed' and 'MemoryLeak: user-profile rss crossed 1.2GiB ... GC cannot reclaim, retained by profileCache' [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- Event MemoryLeakDetected: working set grew 240MB->1.31GB over 90m with no reclaim, 1.2GiB threshold breached [evidence: k8s_events]
- container_oom_events_total=0 — the leak is pre-OOM, the working set is unbounded growth not a one-shot spike [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Not a bad deploy: the working-set ramp began ~84m before the 19:01 rollout, image user-profile:3.4.1 is unchanged from previous revision 11, and the deployment is Available 3/3 — the rollout merely reset one pod's ({{POD3}}) lower memory baseline [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_deployments, k8s_events]
- Not saturation/load: request_rate fell back to 45 rps yet RSS keeps climbing at the same slope, so growth tracks cumulative requests, not concurrency [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Not node_failure: {{NODE}} Ready=True, MemoryPressure=False, 71% allocatable memory free; only {{SVC}} pods show the ramp so the leak is container-scoped [evidence: k8s_node_health]
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- {{SVC}} appends each request's profile to an unbounded profileCache with no eviction -> retained objects keep the heap above its high-water mark so GC cannot reclaim -> container_memory_working_set_bytes grows ~12MB/min from 240MB toward the 1536Mi limit -> the 200MB SLO is breached and the pod is on a trajectory to OOMKill; restarting the deployment reclaims the leaked heap immediately as a stopgap while a code fix (bounded cache) is shipped.
